title: chelone glabra subsp. elongata
intro: Chelone glabra subsp. elongata, also known as the elongated turtlehead, is a plant species belonging to the borer family (Boraginaceae) and is native to southeastern United States. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Chelone glabra subsp. elongata include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate menstrual cramps. Chelone glabra subsp. elongata can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture to utilize its medicinal properties and health benefits.
